# bc — Multi-Agent Orchestration Platform

> bc is a CLI-first, agent-agnostic orchestration platform for AI coding agents. It coordinates teams of AI agents across isolated git worktrees with persistent memory, structured communication channels, and per-agent cost controls.

## Key Features

- **Multi-Agent Orchestration**: Run 5-10 AI coding agents in parallel, each on its own isolated git worktree branch
- **Channel Communication**: Slack-like channels (#eng, #pr, #standup) for structured agent-to-agent coordination
- **Persistent Memory**: Agents accumulate learnings and experiences that persist across sessions
- **Cost Controls**: Per-agent token tracking, budgets, alerts, and automatic hard stops
- **Scheduled Tasks (Demons)**: Cron-powered automation for tests, deploys, and reports
- **14-View TUI Dashboard**: Real-time monitoring of agents, channels, costs, memory, and queues
- **55 CLI Commands**: Full lifecycle management across 9 categories

## Supported AI Tools

bc works with any CLI-based AI coding assistant:
- Claude Code
- Cursor
- GitHub Copilot
- Gemini
- Aider
- OpenCode
- Cline
- Any custom CLI tool

## Pages

- [Home](https://bc-infra.com/): Overview and product introduction
- [Product](https://bc-infra.com/product): Detailed feature breakdown with 55 commands across 9 categories
- [Documentation](https://bc-infra.com/docs): CLI reference, configuration, and quick start guide
- [Waitlist](https://bc-infra.com/waitlist): Sign up for early access to the open beta
- [Privacy Policy](https://bc-infra.com/privacy): Data collection and usage practices
- [Terms of Service](https://bc-infra.com/terms): Usage terms and conditions

## Quick Start

```bash
bc init --preset small-team
bc up
bc home
```
